title: Crispy Low-Carb Tunisian Banana Dumplings
description: A warm, fragrant Tunisian-inspired dessert featuring sweet bananas enveloped in a low‑carb spiced batter, crisped to golden perfection.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together almond flour, coconut flour, semolina, cinnamon, cardamom, salt, and orange zest.
Add olive oil, honey, and lemon juice to the dry mix; stir until a thick dough forms.
Gently fold in the banana slices, ensuring each piece is coated in the batter.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and melt the ghee.
Drop spoonfuls of the banana‑batter mixture into the hot skillet, flattening slightly with a spatula.
Cook each dumpling for 2–3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crisp.
Transfer cooked dumplings to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
Serve warm, optionally dusted with a sprinkle of additional cinnamon or a drizzle of extra honey.
For a spicy twist, sprinkle a pinch of cayenne pepper over the finished dumplings.
Enjoy immediately for the best texture, or store in an airtight container for up to 2 days.
